page: The Vultr voc-c-32c-64gb-500s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server powered by an AMD EPYC processor on the x86_64 architecture. It features 32 vCPUs mapped from 16 physical cores with 2 threads per core under a shared CPU allocation model. The processor operates at a base speed of 2.0 GHz and includes 32 MB of L3 cache. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and includes 500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. A single complimentary public IPv4 address is included with the instance.

Performance benchmarks demonstrate strong capabilities, particularly in compute-heavy tasks. The single-core CPU performance via stress-ng ranks in the top tier (top 10%), while multi-core stress-ng and PassMark CPU scores place in the strong category (top 25%). Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and PassMark CPU encryption tests also rank in the top 25%. Memory performance is solid, with PassMark memory scores in the top 25%, though raw memory bandwidth tests fall into the average range. For machine learning workloads, text generation using a small 135M model achieves top-tier speeds, while larger 7B model processing remains average.

Given the shared CPU allocation and the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this server offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-intensive workloads that do not require dedicated CPU cores. The inclusion of 500 GB of NVMe SSD storage provides fast local I/O. It is suitable for multi-threaded data processing, software compilation, web serving, and moderate database operations.
description: The Vultr voc-c-32c-64gb-500s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring an AMD EPYC processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores on an x86_64 architecture. Operating at 2.0 GHz with a shared CPU allocation, the system is paired with 64.0 GB of system memory and 500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Benchmark results show top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong multi-core capabilities, with PassMark CPU and encryption scores ranking in the top 25%. Memory performance is also strong, though raw bandwidth remains average. The processor supports advanced vector extensions including AVX-512. This configuration delivers a cost-effective resource density for compute-heavy applications. It is well-suited for multi-threaded decompression, web serving, and moderate database workloads.
og_description: Vultr voc-c-32c-64gb-500s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server with 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 500 GB NVMe storage, delivering strong multi-core and top-tier single-core CPU performance.
meta_description: Vultr voc-c-32c-64gb-500s-amd features 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 500 GB NVMe storage, offering strong multi-core compute performance.
tagline: An AMD EPYC-powered compute instance featuring thirty-two vCPUs, sixty-four gigabytes of RAM, and fast local NVMe SSD storage.

64.0 GB of system memory and 500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core and strong multi-core CPU benchmark performance
Shared CPU allocation model optimized for cost-efficient compute density
Suitable for multi-threaded decompression, web serving, and database tasks
